phrases.intent_en: Confirm that online ticket booking is required
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'il faut absolument réserver ses billets en ligne à l'avance ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess keel foh ab-soh-loo-MAHN ray-zur-VAY say bee-YEH ahn LEEN ah lah-VAHNS
phrases.cultural_note: Online booking is strongly recommended — tickets often sell out weeks in advance and same-day tickets are rarely available. The entrance is at Place Denfert-Rochereau in the 14th arrondissement. Without a ticket, the wait can be 2-4 hours.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how far you walk underground
phrases.text_local: Quelle est la longueur du parcours souterrain ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh lah lohn-GUR doo par-KOOR soo-tuh-REHN
phrases.cultural_note: The official tour covers approximately 2 kilometers underground at a depth of about 20 meters. Visitors descend 131 steps to reach the ossuary. The temperature stays a constant 14°C (57°F) year-round — bring a light jacket even in summer.

phrases.intent_en: Warn about the cold temperature underground
phrases.text_local: Il fait assez froid dans les catacombes — avez-vous quelque chose à vous couvrir ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel feh ah-SEH frwah dahn lay kah-tah-KOHM — ah-VAY voo kel-kuh SHOHZ ah voo koo-VREER
phrases.cultural_note: The underground temperature is a constant 14°C regardless of the season. Many visitors are caught off guard after a hot summer day. The walls are lined with the remains of approximately 6 million Parisians, transferred from overcrowded city cemeteries between 1786 and 1860.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how many people's remains are in the catacombs
phrases.text_local: Combien de restes humains se trouvent dans les catacombes ?
phrases.pronunciation: kohm-BYEHN duh REST oo-MEHN suh TROOV dahn lay kah-tah-KOHM
phrases.cultural_note: The ossuary holds the remains of approximately 6 million people. The bones were transferred from overflowing Parisian cemeteries, particularly from the Saints-Innocents cemetery near Les Halles, beginning in 1786. The arrangement of skulls and bones into decorative patterns was done by Inspector Héricart de Thury in the early 19th century.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if photography is allowed inside
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que les photos sont autorisées à l'intérieur ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kuh lay FOH-toh sohn oh-toh-ree-ZAY ah lan-tay-RYUR
phrases.cultural_note: Photography without flash is permitted throughout the catacombs. Tripods are not allowed. The most photographed section is the main entrance archway which reads: 'Arrête! C'est ici l'empire de la Mort' — 'Stop! This is the empire of Death.'

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the visit is suitable for people with claustrophobia
phrases.text_local: Cette visite est-elle adaptée aux personnes claustrophobes ?
phrases.pronunciation: set vee-ZEET eh-TEL ah-dap-TAY oh per-SOHN klohs-troh-FOB
phrases.cultural_note: The tunnels are narrow and low in places. People with severe claustrophobia, heart conditions, or mobility issues are advised to skip this visit. There is no elevator — the descent is 131 steps down and the exit is 83 steps up at a different location from the entrance.
